note: Thin library submodule registering the C3.js Chart backend for the charts module. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I want my Charts module charts to render with C3.js. Which submodule do I enable and what library id does it register?
evals.expected_output: Enable charts_c3; it registers the Chart library plugin id `c3` (C3.js), then select C3 as the library.

evals.prompt: Which external JavaScript libraries does charts_c3 require?
evals.expected_output: C3.js 0.7.20 and D3 7.9.0 (C3 is a D3-based chart library), loaded from CDN by default.

evals.prompt: How do I serve the C3.js and D3 files locally instead of from a CDN with charts_c3?
evals.expected_output: Disable the CDN option in Charts advanced settings and install c3/d3 into /libraries via Composer (c3js/c3, d3/d3) or the npm libraries:copy script.

evals.prompt: In a Charts render array, how do I force a chart to use C3.js?
evals.expected_output: Set '#chart_library' => 'c3' on the chart element (or select C3 in the Views style / block / field, or as the site default library).

evals.prompt: How does the C3.js charts backend relate to Billboard.js in the Charts module?
evals.expected_output: Billboard.js is a fork of C3.js; both are D3-based, so charts_c3 and charts_billboard behave similarly.
